qa_pairs.question: How do you connect to a Jupyter Notebook running on a compute node from a Windows machine using MobaXTerm?
qa_pairs.answer: First, open a Terminal tab in MobaXTerm (Session 1), connect to a cluster, and start your Jupyter Notebook, noting the generated URL which includes the hostname:port and token. Then, open a second Terminal tab (Session 2), and execute the command `ssh -L 8888:<hostname:port> <username>@<cluster>.alliancecan.ca`, replacing the bracketed placeholders with your specific values. Finally, open your web browser and navigate to `http://localhost:8888/?token=<token>`, substituting `<token>` with the value from Session 1.

qa_pairs.question: When using MobaXTerm on Windows to connect to a Jupyter Notebook, what specific details are required to construct the SSH tunnel command in the second terminal session?
qa_pairs.answer: To construct the SSH tunnel command (`ssh -L 8888:<hostname:port> <username>@<cluster>.alliancecan.ca`) in the second MobaXTerm terminal session, you need the `hostname:port` part of the Jupyter Notebook URL obtained from Session 1, your `<username>`, and the `<cluster>` name you initially connected to.
